notes: Champaran. India's first Civil Disobedience movement, launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1917, was prompted by Pandit Raj Kumar Shukla, who urged him to address the plight of Indigo History farmers. Mahatma Gandhi organised the Kheda Satyagraha (1918) in Gujarat. Gandhi also led the Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918) after returning from South Africa. The Bardoli Satyagraha (1928) was led by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel for the farmers of Bardoli.

Lord Irwin (1926-1931). Other Events During his tenure: Simon Commission to India (1927); Harcourt Butler Indian States Commission (1927); Nehru Report (1928); Deepavali Declaration (1929); Lahore session of the Congress (Purna Swaraj Resolution) 1929; First Round Table Conference (1930); Gandhi-Irwin Pact (1931). Mahatma Gandhi led the Dandi March (Salt March) in 1930, accompanied by a group of 78 followers, as a nonviolent protest against the British salt monopoly. Q.632. In which year was the Gandhi-Irwin Pact signed ? SSC CGL 13/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) 1935 (b) 1929 (c) 1931 (d) 1941 Sol.632.(c) 1931. The Gandhi-Irwin Pact was signed on March 5, 1931, between Mahatma Gandhi and Lord Irwin, the Viceroy of India (1926-1931). The pact ended the Civil Disobedience Movement, initiated by Gandhi with the Salt March, and was signed before the Second Round Table Conference in London. Q.633. In the second Round Table Conference, Mahatma Gandhi represented which of the following parties? SSC CGL 17/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) Communist Party of India (b) Indian National Congress (c) All India Forward Bloc (d) Muslim League Sol.633.(b) Indian National Congress. Mahatma Gandhi attended only the Second Round Table Conference in London in September 1931, presided over by Ramsay MacDonald. The Conference in London was inconclusive, so Gandhiji returned to India and resumed civil disobedience. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru attended all three Round Table Conferences.

South Africa. The Natal Indian Congress, founded by Mahatma Gandhi in 1894, aimed to combat discrimination against Indians in South Africa. Gandhiji, born on 2 October 1869, returned to India on 9 January 1915. Q.635. Which important event that started in 1930 played a significant role in motivating and mobilizing Indians to join the non - violent movement for independence? SSC CHSL 10/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Salt Satyagraha (b) Quit India Movement (c) Gadar Movement (d) Swadeshi movement Sol.635.(a) Salt Satyagraha. The Dandi March, or Salt Satyagraha, was initiated by Mahatma Gandhi from Sabarmati Ashram on March 12, 1930, and lasted until April 5, 1930, reaching Dandi (Navsari district). C. Rajagopalachari organized the Vedaranyam Salt March along the east coast, while Gandhi led the march on the west coast. The protest was against the British salt tax, and Gandhi defied it by collecting natural salt from the shore. In the Salt Satyagraha, Sarojini Naidu persuaded Mahatma Gandhi to allow women to join the movement.

Raj Kumar Shukla. The local Congress leaders Brajkishore Prasad and Raj Kumar Shukla first met Mahatma Gandhi at the Lucknow session of the Indian National Congress in 1916 and invited Mahatma Gandhi to have first-hand experience of the plight of Champaran peasants. Gandhi arrived in Champaran on April 15, 1917, to address the grievances of the peasants who were forced to grow indigo under oppressive conditions.

Gandhi-Ambedkar Pact. The Communal Award was announced by the British Prime Minister, Ramsay MacDonald, on 16 August 1932, based on the report of the Indian Franchise Committee (Lothian Committee). This award accorded separate electorates for Muslims, Europeans, Sikhs, Indian Christians, Anglo-Indians, Depressed classes (78 seats), and even to the Marathas for some seats in Bombay. Gandhi - Ambedkar Pact is also known as the Poona Pact. Madan Mohan Malaviya signed it on behalf of Gandhi. It provided for the Joint electorate, reserved seats (147) for depressed classes and 18% seats reserved for the central legislature.

Gorakhpur. Chauri Chaura, a town in Uttar Pradesh was the site of a violent clash on February 4, 1922, during the Non-Cooperation Movement. Protestors set a police station on fire, resulting in the deaths of 22 policemen inside. Q.639. The result of which of the following movements/satyagrahas was the abolishment of the Tinkathia System under which the farmers were asked to cultivate indigo in 3/20th of their holdings ? Graduate Level 21/06/2024 (Shift - 3) (a) Khilafat Movement (b) Kheda Satyagraha (c) Champaran Satyagraha (d) Ahmedabad Satyagraha Sol.639.(c) Champaran Satyagraha (1917) was Gandhi's first protest in India against forced indigo farming. Brajkishore Prasad and Raj Kumar Shukla first met Mahatma Gandhi at the Lucknow session of the Indian National Congress in 1916 and invited him to have first-hand experience of the plight of Champaran peasants.

The resolution for Poorna Swaraj (complete independence) was passed at the Lahore session of the Indian National Congress in December 1929, under the leadership of Jawaharlal Nehru. He hoisted the Indian tricolor flag on the banks of the Ravi river. The non-cooperation movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 to persuade Indians to withdraw their cooperation from the British government and advocate for self-governance.

The Quit India Movement (also known as the August Movement) was launched by the Indian National Congress on August 8, 1942. Other key movements by Mahatma Gandhi include the Civil Disobedience Movement (1930), the Kheda Satyagraha (1918) in Gujarat, demanding relaxation in revenue collection for peasants, and the Champaran Satyagraha (1917), opposing the Tinkathia system, which forced peasants to grow indigo on 3/20th of their land.
Indulal Yajnik. Mahatma Gandhi returned to India on 9 January 1915.
Gopal Krishna Gokhale. He was a mentor to both Mahatma Gandhi and Mohammed Ali Jinnah.

1869. Mahatma Gandhi: Born on 2nd October (observed as International Day of Non-violence). He came back to India on 9th January (celebrated as ‘NRI day’ or ‘Pravasi Bhartiya Divas’ since 2003) 1915 from South Africa.

Mahatma Gandhi. Organisation Founded by MK Gandhi: Majdoor Mahajan (1916) in Ahmedabad. It was related to the labour movement or textile movement in Gujarat. All-India Spinners Association (1925), All-India Village Industries Association (1934). Q.646. The famous scientist, Albert Einstein was inspired by whose idea on non-violence? SSC CGL 18/07/2023 (4th shift) (a) Mahatma Gandhi (b) Vardhaman Mahavira (c) Gautam Buddha (d) B.R. Ambedkar Sol.646.(a) Mahatma Gandhi. His Ideas - Truth and nonviolence, Satyagraha, Sarvodaya (Progress of All), Swaraj (self-rule), Trusteeship (Wealthy people to look after the welfare of the people). Q.647. Who among the following returned his/her medal of Kaisar-i-Hind in 1920 ? Higher Secondary 27/06/2023 ( Shift - 3) (a) S Subramania iyer (b) Sarojini Naidu (c) Rabindranath Tagore (d) Mahatma Gandhi Sol.647.(d) Mahatma Gandhi. Kaiser - e - Hind title was given to Mahatma Gandhi by Governor-General of India Lord Hardinge in 1915 for his services during the Boer War (1899 - 1902). Gandhi returned his title in response to the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre (13th April 1919). Rabindranath Tagore returned his Knighthood title in response to the massacre. Q.648. In 1918, Mahatma Gandhi successfully led a strike of ________. SSC MTS 09/05/2023 (Evening) (a) Army Soldiers (b) Dalits (c) Mill workers (d) Traders Sol.648.(c) Mill workers. Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918) - This is considered the first hunger strike led by Gandhi. Q.649. In Kheda, Gujarat, Patidar peasants organised non-violent campaigns against which of the following demand of the British? SSC CHSL 14/03/2023 (2nd Shift) (a) Low labour rates (b) Long working hours (c) High land revenue (d) Low price for crops Sol.649.(c) High land revenue. Kheda Satyagraha of 1918 was a satyagraha movement in the Kheda district of Gujarat in India organized by Mahatma Gandhi during the period of the British Raj. The peasant-Patidar community of Kheda, who refused to accept a 23 percent tax increase imposed on them despite a devastating crop failure and an outbreak of plague and cholera, served as the focal point of this satyagraha.

September 1932. Poona Pact - Agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and Dr. B. R. Ambedkar - Seats reserved for the depressed classes were increased from 71 to 147 in provincial legislatures and 18 percent of the total in the Central Legislature.
46. Gandhiji (born on 2nd October 1869 in Porbandar, Gujarat) went to South Africa in 1893 as a lawyer, settling first in Durban before moving to Johannesburg.

Mahatma Gandhi. His other Famous Quotes: “A man is but the product of his thoughts. What he thinks, he becomes.”, “Nobody can hurt me without my permission.” Q.653. In which of the following years, did Subhas Chandra Bose refer to Mahatma Gandhi as the “Father of the Nation”? SSC CGL 12/12/2022 (2nd Shift) (a) 1942 (b) 1944 (c) 1939 (d) 1941 Sol.653.(b) 1944. Subhas Chandra Bose called Mahatma Gandhi the ‘Father of the Nation’ in a radio address from Singapore on 6 July 1944.

Delhi. In early 1919, the All India Khilafat Committee was formed under the leadership of the Ali brothers (Maulana Muhammad Ali and Shaukat Ali), Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, Ajmal Khan and Hasrat Mohani, to force the British Government to change its attitude to Turkey.

Mahatma Gandhi. Rabindranath Tagore called Subhash Chandra Bose a 'Desh Nayak'. The famous slogan given by Subhas Chandra Bose: “Dilli Chalo, Jai Hind”, "Give me blood, I will give freedom". Subhas Chandra Bose Autobiography: “The Indian Struggle”.

The Hunter Commission came to India in under the leadership of William Hunter to investigate the Jallianwala Bagh massacre. Another Hunter Commission was on Indian Education, prepared in 1882 by William Wilson Hunter.

Mahatma Gandhi started the Non- Cooperation Movement on September 1920. Lord Chelmsford was the viceroy of British India during the Non-Cooperation Movement. The Chauri -Chaura incident was the driver of subsequent withdrawal of the Non -Cooperation Movement.

1934. It began with the famous Dandi March of Gandhiji, on 12 March 1930. Q.659. Who among the following freedom fighters hoisted the Indian flag at the Gowalia Tank Maidan in Mumbai during the Quit India Movement ? SSC CGL 13/04/2022 (Afternoon) (a) Aruna Asaf Ali (b) Sarojini Naidu (c) Tara Rani Srivastava (d) Matangini Hazra Sol.659.(a) Aruna Asaf Ali. Aruna Asaf Ali popularly known as the 'Grand Old Lady' of the Independence Movement.

Kushal Konwar. Facts of Quit India Movement - The Quit India Resolution was passed by the Congress Working Committee on 8 August 1942 in Bombay. ‘Quit India’ or ‘Bharat Chodo 'slogan was given in this movement . Gandhiji gave the slogan to the people – ‘Do or die’; It is also known as the India August Movement or August Kranti.

Indian Opinion. The Indian Opinion was an important newspaper established in in Durban, South Africa, by Mahatma Gandhi and M. H. Nazar. It was a primary communication organ of the Natal Indian Congress.

Tamil Nadu. Salt March (Dandi Satyagraha, 12th March 1930 to 6th April, 1930 ) was an act of nonviolent civil disobedience led by Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi. Other leaders - K. Kelappan (Malabar), Gopabandhu Chaudhary (Orissa). Expansion of British Rule Q.663. Which of the following personalities had the key role in fighting against the British East India Company in the Battle of Porto Novo? SSC MTS 28/10/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Hyder Ali (b) Maharaja Ranjit Singh (c) Alamgir-II (d) Gopala Nayaker Sol.663.(a) Hyder Ali. The Battle of Porto Novo (1781) was fought between Hyder Ali and the East India Company (EIC), led by Sir Eyre Coote. Hyder Ali was a key figure in the Anglo-Mysore Wars. The First Anglo-Mysore War (1767–1769) was fought between Hyder Ali and the EIC. The Second Anglo-Mysore War (1780–1784) also saw Hyder Ali leading the Mysore forces; however, he died during the conflict, after which his son, Tipu Sultan, continued the war.
